successCriteria.description: src/modules/notes/notes.model.ts exists and exports Note, NoteStatus, CreateNoteDto, and createNoteSchema
src/modules/notes/notes.repository.ts exists and exports INotesRepository and PostgresNotesRepository
PostgresNotesRepository implements INotesRepository and uses pool from src/shared/db/connection.ts
Repository findAll() returns only notes with status 'ACTIVE' ordered by created_at DESC
Repository findById() returns a note only if status is 'ACTIVE'
Repository create() inserts a new note with status 'ACTIVE' and returns the created note
All database queries use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection
No new npm dependencies are added
tests/unit/modules/notes/notes.repository.test.ts exists with Jest tests covering findAll, findById, create, and error scenarios
ARCHITECTURE.md is updated to document the notes module, Note entity, and NoteStatus enum

constraints: Repository pattern: All database access goes through repository interfaces — PostgresNotesRepository is the sole data-access surface for notes
Input validation: createNoteSchema (zod) validates title and body at the boundary before persistence
No sensitive data in logs: Note title and body must not be logged at any level that could expose PII
Error handling: All async repository methods wrap pg Pool calls in try/catch and throw typed AppError instances — no unhandled promise rejections
No new npm dependencies: zod and pg are already project dependencies; no additional packages may be added
No @gestalt/* packages: Do not add @gestalt/* as project dependencies — these are Gestalt platform internals not available on npm
outOfScope: Phase 2: Notes service interface (INotesService) and implementation
Phase 3: Fastify controller, routes, and module barrel export for POST /notes
Any HTTP API endpoints or route registration
RBAC enforcement on note operations (deferred to controller/routes phase)
Audit record creation for note state changes (deferred to service layer)
Note status transitions beyond the initial ACTIVE default (ARCHIVED handling deferred)
Any cross-module dependencies on employee, leave, balance, policy, or notification modules
Database migration SQL — the CREATE TABLE statement in the architecture is informational; migration file creation is deferred
